landing.heroSubtitle: A React 19 + Tailwind v4 design system that doesn't shout. Token-driven, three themes, a framework-agnostic adapter — every detail considered, none of them loud.

quickStart.title: See it in code
quickStart.subtitle: Install the package, import a component, theme it with tokens. The same CodeBlock you see here is a YunUI pattern.

why.title: Care, in the details.
why.subtitle: Not the most components — the most considered ones.
why.componentsDesc: Primitives, page-level patterns, and AI product components — composed, typed, and quietly ready.
why.themesDesc: Token-driven theming. Light, zinc-dark, and a true-black OLED theme — swap with one class.
why.adaptersDesc: Bring your own Link, Image, and i18n. Inject them once; the library stays portable across apps.
why.releasesDesc: Published to npm via trusted publishing with provenance — no long-lived tokens in CI.
why.testsDesc: Strict TypeScript, JSDoc on the public surface, and a test suite that guards every primitive.
why.productionDesc: Quietly running in a real, shipping app — Yunxin, end to end.

livePreview.title: Theming, live
livePreview.subtitle: Real YunUI components — flip the theme switch (top-right) to see light, zinc-dark, and true-black update in place.
livePreview.proPlanDesc: Everything in Basic, plus higher limits and priority support.

faq.title: Questions, answered
faq.subtitle: The things people ask before adopting YunUI in a real project.
faq.a1: Everything is driven by CSS custom-property tokens. Import the stylesheet, then toggle a theme class (light / dark / true-black) on the root html element. Override any token in your own CSS and every component follows — no prop drilling, no theme objects to thread through the tree.
faq.q2: Does it need a specific framework?
faq.a2: No. The core primitives are framework-agnostic. Routing, image, and i18n concerns are injected through a thin adapter layer (YunUIProvider) — wire in Next's Link/Image or your own, and the library stays portable across apps.
faq.a3: Changes live in one library, versioned and pulled in through npm. Releases are published with provenance via tokenless OIDC trusted publishing — no long-lived secrets in CI.
faq.a4: Interactive primitives build on Radix-style behaviors with keyboard navigation, focus management, and ARIA wired in. Components ship typed and JSDoc'd, and a 141-case test suite guards the public surface.

cta.title: Built quietly. Ready when you are.
cta.subtitle: Browse the gallery, read the docs, or jump straight into the source on GitHub.

showcase.title: Every YunUI component, on one page.
showcase.intro: The full gallery — every exported primitive, form control, overlay, data-display piece, layout chrome, pattern and AI component, each in a live Preview / Code frame. Jump to a category below or scroll the whole thing.

foundations.title: Foundations
foundations.description: The tokens, type scale, surfaces and motion that define the YunUI look.

design.title: Utility Classes
design.description: Apps built on YunUI use these classes directly — the design lives in CSS; components are thin wrappers.

layout.title: Layout & Chrome
layout.description: Navbar, Sidebar and Footer — framed previews of the fixed app chrome.

colorTokens.title: Color tokens
colorTokens.description: CSS variables, theme-aware — they re-map under .dark and .true-black.

typeScale.title: Type scale
typeScale.body: Body — the default paragraph rhythm and color.
typeScale.caption: Caption — secondary supporting text.

dashboard.pageDescription: A page composed from YunUI layout pieces — just like a real product page.

navbar.title: Navbar
navbar.description: Floating top nav; switcher/toggle are slots, links are props.

sidebar.title: Sidebar — with collapse
sidebar.description: Click the toggle to collapse / re-open. nav-item active states included.

footer.title: Footer
footer.description: Sections + social are props; layout and styling are the design system.

capabilitySelector.title: Capability selector (AI)
capabilitySelector.description: Multi-select capability chips for AI models.

providerSelect.title: Provider select — real provider icons (AI)
providerSelect.description: CustomSelect with the bundled provider-icon system; Combobox above also uses it.

dialog.title: Dialog (Radix)
dialog.confirmDescription: This is a Radix-backed dialog styled by YunUI.

confirmModals.title: Confirm modals
confirmModals.description: ConfirmModal plus the DeleteConfirmModal / RegenerateConfirmModal presets — portal-rendered with scroll lock and escape-to-close.
confirmModals.publishMessage: Your draft will be visible to everyone on the next deploy.

cardsBadges.title: Cards & badges
cardsBadges.proDesc: Everything in Basic, plus higher limits.

animatedNumber.title: Animated number
animatedNumber.description: Springs from 0 on mount — re-enter the section to replay.

emptyState.title: Empty state
emptyState.noMessagesDesc: When you get messages they'll show up here.

pageLoader.title: Page loader
pageLoader.description: Full-screen centered loader for route transitions — framed here in a fixed-height stage (its min-h-dvh is capped to the frame).

pageStates.title: Page states
pageStates.description: PageLoadingState · PageErrorState · PageEmptyState — the in-content (not full-screen) variants.
pageStates.emptyDesc: Add a provider to get started.

mediaStates.title: Media states
mediaStates.description: MediaLoadingState · MediaErrorState · MediaEmptyState — used across the media generation pages.
mediaStates.emptyDesc: Your generated images will appear here.

toast.title: Toast (sonner)
toast.description: Themed toast helpers — success / error / info / warning.

mediaPageHeader.title: Media page header
mediaPageHeader.description: Title + sync button + error/stats — used across the media generation pages.

modelCard.title: Model card
modelCard.description: AI model card — icon is a slot, all fields are props (no API/schema coupling).
modelCard.opusDesc: Most capable model for complex reasoning and agentic work.
modelCard.deepseekDesc: Open reasoning model with strong math and code.

aiAvatars.title: AI avatars & type icons
aiAvatars.description: ProviderAvatar / ModelAvatar (rounded, image-backed), ProviderIconImg (short alias) and ModelTypeIcon (capability glyphs).

fumadocsButtons.title: Fumadocs button variants
fumadocsButtons.description: buttonVariants — a class-variance-authority recipe for fumadocs-themed buttons; applied to plain elements via className.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.a1: A versioned React 19 + Tailwind v4 design system you sync across every project.
faq.a2: Bump the yunui version; the design follows.

blogCard.title: Blog card
blogCard.postDescription: One design system, every project in sync.

blogPostHeader.title: Blog post header
blogPostHeader.description: Full article header — category, title, author, date and reading time.
blogPostHeader.postDescription: How the design system is versioned and synced across every project.

blogControls.title: Category filter & pagination
blogControls.description: CategoryFilter + BlogPagination — both controlled; the host owns routing.

codeDemo.title: Code demo (tabbed)
codeDemo.description: CodeDemo — a tabbed CodeBlock preset with Python / Node.js / cURL quick-start snippets.

docsActions.title: Docs page actions
docsActions.description: LLMCopyButton (copy page as Markdown for an LLM) and ViewOptions (raw Markdown / GitHub links) — the fumadocs-styled doc header actions.

accountLocked.title: Account locked card
accountLocked.description: Terminal auth screen (banned / suspended) — presentational; the host owns logout/redirect via onBack.
accountLocked.lockedSubtitle: Your account has been temporarily locked for review.
accountLocked.lockedAppeal: If you think this is a mistake, you can appeal the decision.

fellowsBanner.title: Fellows banner
fellowsBanner.bannerDescription: Selective access for builders and researchers.

errorBoundary.title: Error boundary (catches a crashing child)
errorBoundary.childFine: Child renders fine — click to crash it.
errorBoundary.caughtMessage: The child threw during render — this is the fallback UI.

backgroundEffects.title: Background effects (decorative)
backgroundEffects.description: A subtle dotted radial backdrop — it sits behind your page content (intentionally faint).

markdownRenderer.title: MarkdownRenderer
markdownRenderer.description: Full GFM markdown with math, Shiki code, Mermaid diagrams, GitHub callouts and lazy zoomable images — one string in, rich UI out.

contentCodeBlock.title: CodeBlock
contentCodeBlock.description: Shiki-highlighted code with a filename header, line numbers, highlighted lines and copy. The highlighter loads on demand.

calloutBlock.title: CalloutBlock
calloutBlock.description: GitHub-style admonitions — note, tip, important, warning, caution, success.

mathRenderer.title: MathRenderer
mathRenderer.description: LaTeX via KaTeX — inline or centered block.

mermaidDiagram.title: MermaidDiagram
mermaidDiagram.description: On-demand Mermaid diagrams that re-theme for light / dark.

contentImage.title: ContentImage
contentImage.description: A lazy-loaded, click-to-zoom image for markdown / LLM output — rounded, bordered and lightbox-ready.

chatDemo.title: Chat
chatDemo.description: A ChatHeader, a ChatMessageList of ChatMessage rows (assistant body via MarkdownRenderer), and a controlled ChatComposer. Type a message and press Enter.

generationStats.title: GenerationStats
generationStats.description: A row of metric pills for an assistant reply — output tokens, throughput and latency. Localizable via labels.

content.title: Content
content.description: The content-rendering stack — markdown, code, math, diagrams and callouts for LLM / chat / doc output. Lives on @yuhuanowo/yunui/content with on-demand shiki + mermaid.

chat.title: Chat
chat.description: Presentational, slot-based building blocks for AI chat UIs — a message row, a smart-scroll list, an auto-growing composer and a header shell. Lives on @yuhuanowo/yunui/chat and pairs with the content subpath for markdown bodies.

confirmModal.deleteMessage: Are you sure you want to delete "{item}"? This action cannot be undone.
confirmModal.regenerateMessage: Are you sure you want to regenerate "{item}"? The old value will be immediately invalidated.
